blocks.body: Imperative sentences are sentences that express a command, request, advice, or instruction. They are always addressed to the interlocutor (you) and begin with a verb in the basic form.

{ "title": "10. Brief Synopsis", "items": [ "An imperative sentence expresses a command, request, advice or instruction.", "Structure: Verb + Object (no subject).", "Affirmative form: Open the door. / Be quiet.", "Negative form: Don't + Verb → Don't open it. / Don't be late.", "Polite form: Please sit down. / Sit down, please.", "Let's - for suggestions to the group: Let's go!", "The subject you is not usually used.", "Punctuation: period (.) or exclamation mark (!)." ] }
